or IEEE float 32-bit, mono or stereo) instead of always converting to
stereo 16-bit PCM.

repository.name: move-anything-waveform-editor
repository.description: Waveform Editor tool for Move Everything - trim, gain adjust, normalize, and edit WAV files on the Ableton Move

commit.message:
Refactor DSP to stereo, fix drum preset export, and misc improvements

- Refactor DSP from mono int16 to interleaved stereo int16 (L,R,L,R,...)
- Mono WAV files duplicated to both channels on load
- Support PCM 16-bit, PCM 24-bit, and IEEE float 32-bit input
- WAV output always stereo 16-bit PCM
- Waveform display and zero-crossing use L channel
- Fix DSP export symbol: plugin_init -> move_plugin_init_v2
- Fix build.sh to copy from src/dsp/dsp.so (Docker output path)
- Fix drum preset sampleUri: URL-encode spaces (%20)
- Default auto slice threshold from 5 to 25
- Disable loop playback in slice mode, restore on exit
- Back from record-ready state exits module entirely

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix recording race condition and improve recorded file handling

- Add "stopping" state to poll sampler finalization before loading WAV,
  preventing "No waveform data" error from async stop
- Show "SAVING..." screen while waiting for WAV to finalize
- Track recorded files as unsaved (dirty) with discard-on-exit cleanup
- Add timestamp to edit filenames (sample_edit_0306_1423.wav)
- Clean up recording UI header (remove debug VU display)
- Add dirty param support to DSP plugin

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Remap knobs: E4=vertical scale, E5=gain/threshold, E7/E8=twist actions

- E3: zoom only (removed shift+E3 vertical scale)
- E4: vertical scale for all modes (was shift+E3)
- E5: gain/normalize in trim/loop, threshold in slice auto (was E4)
- E7 touch+twist: toggle loop on/off
- E8 touch+twist: snap to zero crossing
- Twist prompts stay visible while knob is touched
- Add snapToZeroCrossing() helper function
- Recording waveform: scrolling write-head visualization
- Recording stop: poll for sampler finalization before loading
- Shift+pad preview: 5% of selection, clamped 0.25s-5s
- Update help.json for new knob layout

commit.message:
Rebrand to scrape-cn: 3-tier strategy, Playwright optional

Major changes:
- Rename to scrape-cn, class ChinaScraper → ScrapeCN
- 3-tier strategy: HTTP fetch (zero deps) → Playwright → CDP Chrome
- Playwright is now a peer dependency (optional)
- New lib/http.js: fetchText, fetchJSON, parseLinks, parseJsonLD,
  parseInlineJSON — works without any browser
- Static methods ScrapeCN.fetch() and ScrapeCN.api() for zero-dep usage
- Rewrite README as a star-worthy tool page with feature matrix,
  strategy comparison, and concise API docs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: scrape-cn
repository.description: 专为中国大陆网站设计的数据抓取工具包 | Web scraping toolkit for Chinese websites — SPA rendering, anti-bot bypass, API interception

commit.message:
v3: Rewrite as reusable scraping toolkit for Chinese websites

Transform from a guide/tutorial into a library that others can directly use.

New architecture:
- lib/scraper.js: ChinaScraper class with 4 extraction strategies
  (auto, search box, API intercept, declarative)
- lib/presets.js: 13 battle-tested site configs (ByteDance, Tencent,
  Bilibili, Feishu portals, Boss直聘, etc.)
- index.js: clean entry point

API: scraper.scrape(target, options) handles the full pipeline:
  navigate → wait → search → click → extract → paginate → dedup

Old guide moved to docs/how-it-works.md.
Old scripts moved to examples/ with 6 new example files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Task 39: feat: add ~/.local/bin to plist template PATH as static entry (#43)

* feat: add ~/.local/bin to plist template PATH as static entry

Add __HOME__/.local/bin to the plist template PATH between
__AUTOPILOT_BIN_DIR__ and /opt/homebrew/bin, ensuring ~/.local/bin is
always searched even if auto-detection fails or plists are created
manually.

- Add __HOME__ placeholder to template and substitution in _substitute_plist()
- Simplify _build_claude_path_entries() by removing fallback logic (now static)
- Add ~/.local/bin to static_dirs check to prevent duplicate PATH entries
- Add tests verifying template contains __HOME__/.local/bin and generated
  plist PATH includes the substituted value
- Update marker-check test to also catch unsubstituted __HOME__

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add __HOME__ marker check to symlink test

The symlink test only checked for __AUTOPILOT_ and __CLAUDE_ prefixed
markers, missing the new __HOME__ marker. A substitution failure
specific to the symlink path would go undetected.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
fix: add JsonProperty annotations to Interview computed methods

Jackson was not serializing requiresFeedback(), canBeRescheduled(),
canBeCancelled(), canBeStarted(), and canBeCompleted() because they
don't follow the JavaBean getter naming convention. This caused the
"Add Feedback" button to never appear on completed interviews.

commit.message:
fix: replace import.meta.dirname with Node 18 compatible __dirname

Critical fix for Railway deployment:
- Railway ignores nixpacks.toml nixPkgs and uses Node 18.20.8
- import.meta.dirname is undefined in Node 18 (requires 20.11+)
- Replaced with classic path.dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url))

Changes:
- server/_core/vite.ts: Add fileURLToPath import
- server/_core/vite.ts: Define __dirname manually for Node 18 compat
- server/_core/vite.ts: Replace all import.meta.dirname → __dirname

This fix works on both Node 18 and Node 20, making it future-proof
and Railway-compatible regardless of environment configuration.

Tested: Server starts without ERR_INVALID_ARG_TYPE errors

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Refactor to two-tier specialist agent architecture for document classification

Router (Sonnet, fast) classifies documents, then specialist agents validate + extract:
- poSpecialist: validates PO classification + extracts line items in one Opus call
- shippingSpecialist: validates shipping + extracts container/vessel data in one Sonnet call
- validateDocumentType: lightweight validator for artwork/certificate/finaldoc/commission
- Removed old validatePOvsPI function (superseded by specialists)
- downloadAndClassify now does router-only classification (no validation step)

commit.message:
feat(dx): add with-secret.sh for autonomous secrets retrieval

Adds scripts/with-secret.sh wrapper that fetches secrets from AWS
Secrets Manager and injects them as env vars before executing a
command. Supports plain strings (-e VAR=secret-id) and JSON field
extraction (-e VAR=secret-id:.field). Multiple -e flags can be
chained.

Updates CLAUDE.md and preflight hook to document the pattern.

commit.message:
Fix kubectl version to exact tag to avoid git timeout

The kubectl-helm-minikube feature resolves partial versions via
git ls-remote to github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes, which times out
from within the cluster. Using the exact 1.35.0 tag skips the lookup.

commit.message:
feat: immediate backfill slot offering when patient joins waitlist

- When patient says LISTA, system now checks for unfilled cancelled
  appointments and offers them immediately (prioritized over calendar gaps)

commit.message:
Use POEM consensus ranking for name scoring

Replace Levenshtein-based name score with POEM rank-based scoring.
All 6 distance measures are used to rank every colour name by
similarity to the guess. The target's rank position is converted
to a score via a quadratic curve: round((1 - rank/(N-1))^2 * 100).

This means word order no longer matters — "gray sky" scores 100
against "Sky Gray" instead of ~56. Typos are also more forgiving
since multiple measures can compensate for character-level errors.

The p=2 curve rewards the top positions while heavily penalising
distant matches: top 1% → 98, top 10% → 81, top 50% → 25.

- Updated about page scoring description and formula
- Updated CLAUDE.md with new scoring documentation

commit.message:
Add -p profile build flag and per-handle cleanup callbacks

Compiler: Add -p flag for profile builds (-O2, frame pointers, no ASAN/LTO).
Validates -p conflicts with -g and --emit-c. Adds SN_PROFILE_CFLAGS support
across all backends (GCC, Clang, TCC, MSVC).

Runtime: Add cleanup_fn field to RtHandleV2 for O(1) per-handle cleanup
registration and lookup, replacing O(n) arena cleanup linked-list traversal.
GC compact and arena destruction both check handle cleanup_fn first.
Clone inherits cleanup_fn from source handle. Arena linked list retained
as fallback for arena-level callbacks (e.g. thread join).

This eliminates the O(n²) bottleneck where every Json wrapper insertion
walked the entire cleanup list (61% CPU in benchmarks → 0%).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use pdfRequestId via RequestContext for deterministic PDF retrieval

Replace takeLatest() with request-scoped pdfRequestId (UUID). The controller
generates it, puts it in RequestContext, the tool reads it and stores the PDF
with that key, the controller retrieves by the same key.

This is the same propagation channel as orgId (proven to work through Mastra's
delegation layers). Deterministic, concurrent-safe, no regex, no LLM dependency.

Edge cases resolved vs takeLatest():
- Concurrent requests: each has its own UUID, no cross-contamination
- Multiple PDFs per conversation: each request-scoped
- Failed requests leaving orphans: keyed entries expire via TTL, never
  accidentally picked up by unrelated requests
